title: Unit Spawning and AI Controller Assignment
interaction_flow: Caller → Units: canCreate(team, type)  // guard: !type.useUnitCap || countType < getCap(team) && !type.isBanned()
Units → TeamData: countType(type)  // returns current unit count for that type on this team
Units → Units: getCap(team)  // returns Integer.MAX_VALUE for wave team; else rules.unitCap + team.data().unitCap
[alt BLOCKED] Units → Caller: return false  // over cap or type banned
[alt ALLOWED] Caller → UnitType: create(team)
UnitType → constructor(Prov<Unit>): get()  // allocates entity e.g. UnitEntity::create
UnitType → UnitComp: unit.team = team
UnitType → UnitComp: setType(this)
UnitComp: maxHealth=type.health, drag=type.drag, armor=type.armor, hitSize=type.hitSize
UnitComp → UnitComp: setupWeapons(type)  // if mounts.length != type.weapons.size
UnitComp: abilities[] = copy of type.abilities
UnitComp (if controller==null) → UnitType: createController(self())  // returns controller.get(unit)
UnitType → AIController (ctor): new GroundAI() / new FlyingAI()  // if AI team or non-playerControllable
UnitType → CommandAI (ctor): new CommandAI()  // if player-controllable team with rtsAi off
AIController (ctor): resetTimers()  // timer.reset(timerTarget, Mathf.random(40f)), timer.reset(timerTarget2, Mathf.random(60f))
UnitComp → UnitComp: controller(newController)
UnitComp: this.controller = next
UnitComp → AIController: unit(self())  // binds unit reference
AIController: this.unit = unit
AIController → AIController: init()  // base: no-op
CommandAI.init(): command = type.defaultCommand ?? type.commands.first() ?? UnitCommand.moveCommand
UnitType → UnitComp: ability.created(unit)  // for each ability
UnitType → UnitComp: unit.ammo = ammoCapacity
UnitType → UnitComp: unit.elevation = flying ? 1f : 0
UnitType → UnitComp: unit.heal()  // full HP
UnitType → Caller: return unit  // NOT yet in world
Caller → UnitComp: unit.set(x, y) + unit.rotation = rotation
Caller → UnitComp: unit.add()  // registers into Groups/EntityGroup
UnitComp → TeamData: updateCount(type, +1)
[alt OVER CAP] UnitComp → Call(RPC): unitCapDeath(self())  // if count() > cap() && useUnitCap && !spawnedByCore
Call → UnitComp: unit.dead = true; Fx.unitCapKill.at(unit); unitDestroy(id)
[alt OK] continue
Caller → Units: notifyUnitSpawn(unit)  // server-only
Units → Call(RPC): unitSpawn(new UnitSyncContainer(unit))  // syncs spawn to clients without waiting for snapshot
--- First update tick (server-side) ---
UnitComp.update() → UnitComp: shouldUpdateController()  // returns true normally
UnitComp → AIController: controller.updateUnit()
AIController: [if useFallback()] fallback.updateUnit()
AIController: updateVisuals()  // wobble if flying; lookAt(prefRotation())
AIController: updateTargeting()  // retarget() timer; findMainTarget(); updateWeapons(); mount.shoot/rotate
AIController: updateMovement()  // implemented by GroundAI/FlyingAI subclasses
UnitComp.update() → UnitComp: [if !controller.isValidController()] resetController()  // fallback: type.createController(self())
key_relationships: UnitType creates Unit via constructor Prov<Unit> (UnitEntity::create, MechUnit::create, etc.)
UnitType.createController delegates to UnitType.controller Func<Unit,UnitController> (default: GroundAI, FlyingAI, or CommandAI)
UnitComp holds UnitController (AIController / CommandAI / Player / LogicAI)
AIController (base) extended by: GroundAI, FlyingAI, BuilderAI, MinerAI, CommandAI, LogicAI, MissileAI, etc.
CommandAI extends AIController — adds command, stances (Bits), commandController
UnitComp.controller(next) calls AIController.unit(unit) which triggers init()
Units.canCreate uses TeamData.countType and Units.getCap for cap enforcement
UnitComp.add() enforces cap again at entity-add time (secondary check)
Units.notifyUnitSpawn calls Call.unitSpawn (RPC) for multiplayer sync

key_methods.Units.getCap(Team): Returns Integer.MAX_VALUE for waveTeam/disableUnitCap; else max(0, unitCap + team.data().unitCap)
key_methods.UnitType.create(Team): constructor.get() → setType → abilities.created → ammo/elevation → heal(). Returns unit (not added).
key_methods.UnitType.spawn(Team, float, float, float: create() + set(x,y) + add() + notifyUnitSpawn. Handles segment chains.
key_methods.UnitType.createController(Unit): controller.get(unit) — default Func: AIteam→aiController.get() else CommandAI
key_methods.UnitComp.setType(UnitType): Sets stats; setupWeapons; copies abilities; if controller==null calls createController
key_methods.UnitComp.controller(UnitController): Sets field, calls controller.unit(self()) if not already bound
key_methods.UnitComp.add(): updateCount(+1); cap death check via Call.unitCapDeath if over cap
key_methods.UnitComp.update(): Delegates to controller.updateUnit() server-side; resets controller if invalid

important_notes: Units.unitSpawn() (@Remote unreliable) is the CLIENT-SIDE sync handler — 'doesn't actually do anything, reading calls add()' — it is NOT the spawn trigger.
Cap is checked TWICE: (1) Units.canCreate() before build starts, (2) UnitComp.add() when entity enters world — the latter kills the unit via Call.unitCapDeath if over cap.
AIController.resetTimers() is called from an instance-initializer block (not constructor body), so it runs for every subclass before init().
CommandAI.init() is invoked via AIController.unit(unit) → init() chain; it sets up the default command/stance.
controller Func<Unit,UnitController> distinguishes player-controllable units: AI teams or non-playerControllable get aiController.get() (GroundAI/FlyingAI); player teams with rtsAi off get CommandAI.
Segment units (e.g. worm-like): UnitType.spawn creates the head via create()+add(), then loops creating child segments also via create()+add() and chains them with addChild().
spawnedByCore flag: units spawned by CoreBlock set this flag; they skip cap-death in add() but are despawned via Call.unitDespawn when not player-controlled.
